name: IRC_CAND_EMAIL_MESSAGES
description: This table stores the emails that are sent and received in the context of an interaction on the Submission, Candidate Pool and Candidate General Profile.

columns.Name: CAND_EMAIL_MESSAGE_ID
columns.Comments: Primary key for the IRC_CAND_EMAIL_MESSAGES table.

columns.Name: AGENT_REVIEWED_STATUS
columns.Comments: Status of the email following the review by the AI Agent.

columns.Name: INTERACTION_ID
columns.Comments: Foreign key into the IRC_INTERACTIONS table. Each message is associated to an interaction in this manner. There can be multiple messages associated to an interaction. All the messages in the interaction in turn represents the conversation.

columns.Name: SENDER_PERSON_ID
columns.Comments: Foreign key into PER_PERSONS. PersonId for the candidate or recruiter/ hiring manager who is sending this message.

columns.Name: SUBJECT
columns.Comments: Subject content of message that will be sent.

columns.Name: BODY_SNIPPET
columns.Comments: Message body snippet for preview on the email summary UI.

columns.Name: MESSAGE_IDENTIFIER
columns.Comments: Denotes a unique Id that is sent along with the message. This is the SMTP Message-Id header for the message sent/received.

columns.Name: IN_REPLY_TO_HEADER
columns.Comments: The In-Reply-To column denotes the unique message Id that the message in reference is a response to.

columns.Name: MESSAGE_DIRECTION
columns.Comments: To distinguish which way the messages are going-- to or from the candidate-- without having to derive the information from SENDER_PERSON_ID and IRC_CAN_MESSAGE_RECIPIENTS. Possible values for this column are ORA_INBOUND or ORA_OUTBOUND.

columns.Name: SEND_TIME
columns.Comments: The time when a message is sent.

columns.Name: ESS_JOB_READ_TIME
columns.Comments: The time taken for the ESS job to be read

columns.Name: DELIVERY_STATUS
columns.Comments: Delivery status of a message that has been sent or received. Different statuses include ORA_UNKOWN, ORA_DELIVERED, ORA_NOT_DELIVERED (at the least these few).

columns.Name: READ_FLAG
columns.Comments: Whether the email has been read by the candidate.

columns.Name: BODY_PLAIN_TEXT
columns.Comments: Stores the Message body in plain text.

columns.Name: MASKED_BODY_TEXT
columns.Comments: Store the body text without PII data

columns.Name: BODY_HTML
columns.Comments: Stores the message body in HTML.

columns.Name: REFERENCES_HEADER
columns.Comments: The References is a store of all the message_ids that are part of the conversation thread for the message sent/received. This is empty for the first message that is sent but should have a value for every subsequent message/interaction in the conversation.  This is important for maintaining the Email Message Headers when replying to  messages from the Messaging UI (this is important for us mimic the behavior of a mail client in order to display a conversation thread in external mailboxes).

columns.Name: DELIVERY_ERROR_MESSAGE
columns.Comments: Error message if the delivery of the message fails.

columns.Name: OBJECT_VERSION_NUMBER
columns.Comments: Used to implement optimistic locking. This number is incremented every time that the row is updated. The number is compared at the start and end of a transaction to detect whether another session has updated the row since it was queried.

columns.Name: CREATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who created the row.

columns.Name: CREATION_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the creation of the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who last updated the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the last update of the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_LOGIN
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the session login associated to the user who last updated the row.
